description: Beginning of a letter from a certain Abū l-Faraj to "the judge" (perhaps Eliyyahu the Judge). Dating: ca. early 13th century. Opening with blessings and ending with various greetings, including to al-Raʾīs R. Avraham (Maimonides, r. 1205–1237 CE), R. Menaḥem, al-Shaykh al-Muhadhdhab, al-Shaykh al-Makīn, and al-Shaykh al-Muhadhdhab Bū Saʿīd. Reused on verso for accounts in Arabic script and Greek/Coptic numerals. On verso mentions Abū l-ʿAlāʾ al-Zajjāj (the glassmaker; possibly the same as in JRL Series A 1043 (PGPID 5207)); it is not entirely clear if this is part of the letter or part of the accounts.
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית מהמאה ה-13. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
